title: Processes and threads - programs that run
author: Prodipta Acharjee
concept: A process is a running program with its own private memory. A thread is a strand of execution inside a process; threads in the same process share its memory.
explanation: A program on disk is just a file; start it and you get a process - a running instance with its own slice of memory, isolated from other processes. Inside one process you can have several threads, each running through the code on its own, but all sharing the same memory. That sharing is what makes threads powerful (easy to cooperate) and dangerous (they can step on each other's data - the source of a whole class of bugs). Doing more than one thing at a time is concurrency.
